calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-20-2026 Continued To Jul-15-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Claim And Delivery (Re-Noticed)
Off Calendar Apr-01-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Prior to Open Court, Ms. Woods appears via Zoom. The Court did not receive any notice to contest and there is no appearance by the other side. At the instruction of the court, the court's tentative ruling will be the final ruling of the court.

No appearances in Open Court. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiff Zahraya T. Woods' Motion for Claim and Delivery is DENIED.

The court does not find Plaintiff has established probable validity of her claim to an exclusive right of possession. The record would support a finding the vehicle was purchased with Plaintiff's funds and Plaintiff was personally involved in the purchase. The parties, however, shared possession of the vehicle, at least up to the second half of December. The vehicle is registered in Defendant's name. On December 29, 2025, the police facilitated a transfer of possession to Defendant. The parties' respective rights of possession are not sufficiently clear to support issuance of a writ providing for Plaintiff's exclusive possession of the vehicle at this time. (Code of Civil Procedure section 512.060(a)(1).)

Second, the record contains no information about the location of the vehicle. If the vehicle is likely in a private place, there is no showing of probable cause to believe the vehicle is located there. (Code of Civil Procedure section 512.060(6).)

Third, even if the record supported issuance of a writ, Petitioner would be required to post an undertaking of $5,000. (Code of Civil Procedure section 512.060(a)(2).)

Fourth, the court is without a proposed order or a proposed writ of possession.

Prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and must drop a hard courtesy copy in Dept. 302's inbox.
Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Claim And Delivery Of Personal Property
Off Calendar Feb-24-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (by Zoom):
Zahraya T. Woods 650-303-6042 Plaintiff in Pro Per.
Tiffany Chew 925-504-7672 Defendant in Pro Per (In-person).

ARGUED. Ms. Chew contests and presents arguments. Ms. Chew states she did not know about this complaint. Ms. Woods presents arguments. The Court comments; complaint not properly served on Defendant; no proof of service on case file, absent proof of service on file, the Court can't make a ruling. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF ZAHRAYA WOODS' Motion For Claim And Delivery Of Personal Property.

Dismissed without Prejudice.

Judge: John M. True, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMT)
